They are evaluating multiple suppliers then going to RFP. 2.27.26 KK - meeting tuesday for discovery
hard.ais_why: The 31 Mar 2026 demo confirmed AI value-prop alignment (consolidated spend analytics, AI-driven CLM, autonomous negotiation) but IT raised a governance constraint requiring AI models to run in a private tenant with no Cinemark data leaving that environment. Buyer questions on AutoClass, Merlin intake via Teams, MS Word InstaReview and Anna autonomous negotiation were active but reactive to the vendor flow; SVP Jon Gappa framed the eval as comparing Zycus against other solutions rather than committing to AI as the differentiator. AI importance rated Neutral, no AI champion identified, sits on the AI Curious side.
hard.competitor: Undecided multi-vendor RFP field (no rival named by buyer); status-quo/no-decision
hard.primary_competitor: Multi-vendor RFP process and status quo (do-nothing)

north_star_verdict.headline: Well-received 31 Mar demo to a 7-person Cinemark committee, but the buyer has gone silent since Leandro Damasio's 4 May email; four rep follow-ups and a 1 Jul call to Ekta Matta drew no reply, the commercial input sheet is overdue since 18 Jun, and no RFP has issued. Momentum is thinning, not dead.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 23 Feb 2026, Qualified 6 Mar, moved to Formal Evaluation 31 Mar after the full demo (~97 days in stage as of 6 Jul). Last GENUINE two-way buyer touch was Leandro Damasio's 4 May email (~63 days ago); every touch since is rep-initiated and unanswered (29 May, 9 Jun emails; 1 Jul Could-Not-Connect call to Ekta Matta). Close date 31 Jul is 25 days out on a Pipeline forecast. Remaining path (return commercial inputs, quantify ROI, approve internal business case, issue and score the RFP, down-select, negotiate, legal/IT/procurement) cannot complete in 25 days for a $150k multi-module platform; realistic close is Q4 2026. Verdict weakening vs prior sweep: buyer silence has stretched from 8 days to ~9 weeks. Single biggest blocker: an unbuilt internal business case with no engaged economic buyer to sponsor budget.
evidence: Strong demo but no buyer meeting since: 31 Mar 90-minute demo with 7 Cinemark stakeholders; John Gappa said it 'definitely covers the very vast majority of our pain points' (Avoma 31 Mar), but no buyer call or reply has landed since Leandro Damasio's 4 May email.
Commercial input sheet stalled: Next Step 12 May 'FU on commercial input sheet' and overdue Deal-Engine tasks 18 Jun show the buyer has not returned commercial inputs Zycus needs to price the deal.
Rep chasing, no buyer response: [Clari] emails 29 May and 9 Jun to Leandro Damasio and a 1 Jul Could-Not-Connect call to Ekta Matta all went unanswered.
Multi-vendor RFP not yet issued: Next Step 6 Mar 'They are evaluating multiple suppliers then going to RFP'; no RFP document has been received.
Business case unbuilt / budget pending: the sourcing team (mostly recent hires) is building a procurement business case to justify budget; ROI never quantified by the buyer.
25-day close (31 Jul) is not credible for RFP completion, pricing finalization, business-case approval and contracting on a $150k multi-module platform.

metrics.narrative: Leandro Damasio articulated clear operational pain across the 6 Mar discovery and 31 Mar demo: fragmented spend across Workday and Microsoft Dynamics forcing manual Excel consolidation, no ready answer to 'what is my contract compliance rate', a manual Smartsheet-based contract process, and heavy vendor bloat (Devika referenced his example of ~300 plumbing suppliers). Duane Flowers confirmed on 31 Mar that obligation tracking is informal ('there's nothing formal at this time... some level of joint ownership'). What is missing is any quantified value case: no savings, cycle-time, or ROI figure was stated by the buyer, and Leandro flagged on discovery that they 'still need to understand the ROI' on autonomous tail-spend. Quantifying that business case with Leandro and Duane is the ranked move.
sources: Avoma demo 31 Mar (Duane Flowers on informal obligation tracking)
Prior canonical record (Leandro Damasio discovery 6 Mar pain quotes)

economic_buyer.narrative: Jon Gappa, Senior Vice President - Sourcing (OpportunityContactRole), is the inferred economic buyer: on the 31 Mar demo he set the frame ('we're in the process of building a business case around us moving forward with this type stuff... think about the next steps in our proposal process') and owns the approval that spend/CLM tooling would need. He is mapped but NOT engaged: his only touch is the 31 Mar demo, and no follow-up has reached him since. Budget approval is explicitly pending the internal business case. The move is to convert Leandro Damasio's advocacy into direct Jon Gappa sponsorship, using Zycus VP and executive sponsor Alexa Bradley (already on the 31 Mar call) rather than the CEO.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Jon Gappa, Senior Vice President - Sourcing)
Avoma demo 31 Mar (Jon Gappa framing the business case)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are well evidenced from the 31 Mar demo: consolidated, AI-classified spend visibility on top of Workday (not replacing it); AI-driven CLM with Microsoft Word redlining, clause/risk InstaReview, and parallel review; intake orchestration through Microsoft Teams to minimize change management; DocuSign e-signature (Leandro confirmed 'We use Docusign'); autonomous tail-spend negotiation (Anna); and an IT-imposed AI-governance constraint that models run in a private tenant with no Cinemark data leaving it (SF AIS_Why__c). Change management was a stated concern the buyer weighted heavily. Configurability of contract risk scoring by category was a direct John Hunter/Jon Gappa ask on the call. Criteria are confirmed; the open one is the private-tenant AI governance requirement, which is currently exposed.
sources: Avoma demo 31 Mar (DocuSign, Teams/Word integration, risk-scoring configurability)

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence and Next Step: Cinemark ran discovery (3 Mar, 6 Mar) then a full demo (31 Mar) with a broad committee, and stated they are 'evaluating multiple suppliers then going to RFP' (Next Step 6 Mar) with Leandro Damasio confirming a likely formal RFP. Contract development involves category leaders, legal, accounting, tax and risk teams (Jon Gappa, 31 Mar: 'they all play a role in our contract development process'), and approval workflows are amount-tiered (Leandro asked about thresholds where higher-value contracts route to more senior approvers). The process is buyer-owned and RFP-gated; what is unconfirmed is the RFP issue date, scoring criteria, and the internal budget-approval timeline. Securing the RFP timeline in writing is a ranked move.
sources: Avoma demo 31 Mar (Jon Gappa on cross-functional contract review; Leandro on amount-tiered workflows)

paper_process.narrative: This is an early-stage (Formal Evaluation) new-business deal, so contracting mechanics are not yet in play and their absence is normal, not a red flag. What is known about the buyer's contracting environment: heavy use of supplier (vendor) paper today with a stated intent to move toward Cinemark paper (Jon Gappa, 31 Mar: 'today it's been heavily skewed towards supplier paper... in the future we want to move that needle much more tilted towards Cinemark paper'), DocuSign for e-signature, and a cross-functional legal/tax/risk review chain. No MSA, Order Form, SOW, InfoSec review, or procurement channel has been discussed. Visibility will come once a business case is approved and the deal enters commercials; the IT private-tenant AI-governance/security review is the one paper-adjacent item already surfaced.
sources: Avoma demo 31 Mar (supplier vs Cinemark paper; DocuSign)

identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed, multi-faceted pain owned by the sourcing organization: fragmented spend with no central visibility, manual Excel-based analysis, unknown contract-compliance rate, a manual Smartsheet contract repository with missed-renewal / auto-renewal exposure, and tail-spend leakage across hundreds of small suppliers. On the 31 Mar demo the buyer repeatedly validated this ('this definitely resonates with us... it's all a manual process... the way you're demonstrating really flows well' - Jon Gappa). The pain owner is Leandro Damasio (indirect/services spend) with Duane Flowers on COGS/food-and-beverage. Inferred but unconfirmed: the financial magnitude of missed-renewal cost, which Devika raised generically but the buyer never sized. Pain is not the blocker; converting it into a quantified, budget-ready case is.
sources: Avoma demo 31 Mar (Jon Gappa 'it's all a manual process')
Prior canonical record (Leandro Damasio discovery pain quotes 6 Mar)

champion.narrative: Leandro Damasio (Senior Director, Sourcing per his 31 Mar call self-introduction; verified as a Salesforce task/activity contact, ldamasio@cinemark.com) has been the operational champion: he initiated contact, led both discovery calls, brought six additional stakeholders including SVP Jon Gappa to the demo, and detailed requirements unprompted. What has DEVELOPED is genuine advocacy and multi-threading; what is MISSING and now at risk is engagement and conversion to power. Leandro went out of office and has not replied since his 4 May email despite follow-ups on 29 May and 9 Jun, has not returned the commercial input sheet, and has not sponsored Zycus to Jon Gappa as the economic buyer. The champion is cooling and must be re-activated with a specific value artifact before any commercial step.
sources: Avoma demo 31 Mar (Leandro leading the committee)
SF Next_Step__c and Tasks (4 May last reply; 29 May, 9 Jun unanswered follow-ups)

competition.narrative: No competitor has been named by the buyer. Cinemark is running a multi-vendor, RFP-gated benchmarking exercise (Next Step 6 Mar: 'evaluating multiple suppliers then going to RFP'), so the true competitive read is that the field is undecided and the strongest current threat is the buyer's own status-quo/no-decision path: an unbuilt business case and unapproved budget mean the most likely outcome is drift, not a loss to a named rival. Zycus demonstrated fit on every stated criterion and is the furthest-engaged vendor on record. We win by authoring the quantified business case and shaping the RFP criteria around single-platform architecture, Merlin/Word/Teams integration on their exact stack, and autonomous tail-spend negotiation before the RFP commoditizes the comparison. No rival name should be asserted without buyer evidence.
sources: Avoma demo 31 Mar (fit validated across spend, CLM, negotiation)

deal_movement.summary: Peaked at the 31 Mar demo, then decayed: initial SaaS pricing shared and a Horizon invite extended 20 May, but the buyer stopped responding after 4 May and the commercial input sheet has been overdue since 18 Jun. All recent movement is rep-side outreach, not buyer engagement.

Last genuine two-way buyer touch: Leandro Damasio email reply (busy, will follow up with commercial inputs)
Initial SaaS pricing shared and Horizon event invite extended
Commercial input sheet still outstanding; Deal-Engine to-dos flagged overdue
Could-Not-Connect outbound call to Ekta Matta; buyer still silent

competitive_position.summary: No competitor has been named by the buyer as of 6 Jul 2026. Cinemark is benchmarking multiple vendors ahead of a formal RFP (Leandro Damasio, Next Step 6 Mar), so the field is undecided and the single strongest CURRENT threat is the buyer's status-quo / no-decision path: an unbuilt internal business case, unapproved budget, and a recent-hire sourcing team still 'trying to understand what's out there.' Zycus is the furthest-engaged vendor on record with validated fit on every stated criterion. The RFP itself is the competitive risk because it resets the comparison to a feature/price matrix; the counter is to author the business case and shape RFP criteria before issuance. No specific rival (Coupa, Ariba, GEP, etc.) should be asserted without buyer evidence.

The one exposed area is IT's private-tenant AI-governance requirement, and the ROI/value case remains to be proven.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage, multi-threaded deal that has gone quiet; the win levers are re-activating the champion, authoring the business case, and clearing the IT AI-governance constraint.
flags: Convert Leandro Damasio's operational advocacy into direct Jon Gappa (SVP Sourcing) executive sponsorship before the RFP issues, using Alexa Bradley as the Zycus exec sponsor already in the room.

Co-develop the quantified business case (vendor rationalization on 300+ tail suppliers, missed-renewal cost, compliance lift) so Zycus authors the RFP criteria rather than responding to a commodity feature/price matrix.
Address IT's private-tenant AI-governance constraint early; it is a gating requirement for the Merlin/Agentic AI/Anna scope and left unresolved it will stall the AI value story.

champion_strength.summary: Leandro Damasio was a strong, active champion through early May: he initiated contact, led both discovery calls, brought six colleagues including SVP Jon Gappa to the demo, and detailed requirements unprompted. He has since cooled: out of office and unresponsive since his 4 May email, the commercial input sheet unreturned, and no conversion of his advocacy into Jon Gappa's executive sponsorship. What has developed is advocacy and multi-threading; what is missing is current engagement and access to power. He must be re-activated with a specific value artifact before any commercial step.
champion_strength.source: SF task/activity contact (ldamasio@cinemark.com); Avoma demo 31 Mar; Next_Step__c and Tasks (4 May last reply, 29 May/9 Jun unanswered)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS 6.0 / Moderate. The AI capabilities (AutoClass, Merlin intake via Teams, Word InstaReview, Anna autonomous negotiation) demoed well but landed as reactive features inside a generic procurement business case, not as the decisive differentiator; there is no AI champion and IT has imposed a private-tenant governance constraint. Zycus is under-positioned on the agentic story relative to its potential.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c 6.0, AIS_Status__c Moderate. The 31 Mar demo confirmed AI fit but the buyer framed it as comparing Zycus against other solutions rather than committing to AI as the differentiator, with no AI champion and an IT private-tenant governance guardrail. The calls corroborate the field: genuine curiosity, sophisticated questions, but ROI-driven and not AI-led.

customer_preference.evidence: Demo strongly received (Jon Gappa 31 Mar: 'this definitely resonates with us... flows well with how we want to look at our spend') but no decisive Zycus-specific lean, no CPO ask, and the eval is framed as comparing Zycus against other solutions; buyer has since gone quiet.

business_case.evidence: Rich qualitative pain (fragmented spend, unknown compliance rate, manual Smartsheet contracting, ~300 tail suppliers) but no quantified savings, cycle-time, or ROI figure stated; buyer said they 'still need to understand the ROI' and are 'building a business case' with budget pending.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A well-received 31 Mar demo to a 7-person Cinemark committee (including SVP Jon Gappa) established fit, but the buyer has gone silent since Leandro Damasio's 4 May email; the overdue commercial input sheet and an unbuilt internal business case now sit between us and an RFP that has not issued.

win_position.text: Zycus demoed to a 7-person Cinemark committee on 31 Mar and Jon Gappa confirmed it 'covers the very vast majority of our pain points', but no economic buyer has committed and the field is still an open multi-vendor RFP.
The buyer has been silent since Leandro Damasio's 4 May email despite follow-ups on 29 May and 9 Jun, and the commercial input sheet has been overdue since 18 Jun.
No rival is named, but the decision is gated behind an unbuilt internal business case and an RFP that has not issued, a real no-decision / status-quo risk.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has stalled: the last genuine buyer reply was 4 May, and 29 May and 9 Jun rep emails plus a 1 Jul call to Ekta Matta all went unanswered.
Commercial topics did enter the deal (initial SaaS pricing shared and a Horizon invite extended 20 May), but the buyer has not returned the commercial inputs to advance them.

customer_commitment.text: Strong multi-threading (SVP, two Senior Directors, three managers and a Brazil lead on the 31 Mar demo) signals broad organizational interest across the sourcing team.
Cinemark is still comparing Zycus against other tools to build a generic procurement business case rather than committing (per Jon Gappa's framing and the AIS read), with no single sign-off secured.

deal_risk.text: Champion Leandro Damasio has cooled: out of office and unresponsive since early May, and his advocacy has not converted into Jon Gappa's executive sponsorship.
The 25-day close (31 Jul) is not credible with no RFP received, pricing unagreed, and the business case unapproved for a $150k multi-module platform.

deal_momentum.evidence: Buyer silent ~9 weeks; four unanswered rep touches; commercial input sheet overdue since 18 Jun.

customer_commitment.evidence: Broad demo attendance but framed as benchmarking, no Zycus-specific commitment, business case unbuilt.

deal_risk.evidence: Cooling champion, unengaged EB, status-quo/no-decision risk, non-credible close date.

critical_signals.text: The 31 Mar demo landed well with Jon Gappa saying it covers the vast majority of Cinemark's pain, but there has been no buyer meeting since and outreach has thinned to unanswered one-way email.
No vendor has been named; the real threat is Cinemark's own status quo, an unbuilt business case and unapproved budget that could stall the project rather than lose it to a rival.
Initial SaaS pricing was shared 20 May but the commercial input sheet Cinemark owes has been overdue since 18 Jun and no ROI has been quantified.
Lisa Block, Category Manager for Technology, joined on her second day at the 31 Mar demo and is a likely IT/AI-governance evaluator, but has not been engaged since.
